deep_dive.title: An Evidential Reasoning Approach for Aerial Target Classification and Intent Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Timely classification and intent prediction of aerial targets is crucial for a combat aircraft to make informed tactical decisions. The prevailing approach for aerial target classification relies on data-driven models using time-series data. These models perform well with long-duration data; however, this is impractical in combat situations involving rapidly evolving threats that demand quick decisions. Minimizing false predictions is essential, as uncertainty is preferable to incorrect assessments in high-risk environments. Here, we propose an integrated approach to target classification and intent prediction that enables decisions from partial data in settings where threats require rapid response. In the proposed method, predictions are generated from short sequential sub-samples instead of the entire time series, and the results are refined by propagating beliefs across sub-samples. Outputs from classifiers are combined through an evidential reasoning framework to manage uncertainty. Target intent is inferred using rule-based techniques and a distance-based combination method to fuse information over time. Due to lack of publicly available datasets, a dataset for aerial target classification was generated for evaluation. A case study involving eight targets is used to demonstrate the effectiveness of the approach, whereby accuracies of 88% and 93% are achieved for target type classification and intent prediction, respectively.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evidential reasoning approach used for classification and prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用证据推理方法进行目标分类和预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用证据推理方法进行目标分类和预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用证据推理方法进行目标分类和预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an evidential reasoning approach for aerial target classification and intent prediction, enabling decisions from partial data under uncertainty.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对空中目标分类和意图预测问题，现有数据驱动模型需要长时间数据，不适用于快速决策场景。本文提出一种基于证据推理的集成方法，能够在部分数据下进行决策，减少误预测。实验表明该方法在不确定性环境下有效，为证据推理在分类预测中的应用提供了新范例。

quick_skim.title: From Bayes' Rule to Bayes Rules: Optimal Information Processing and Axiomatic Foundations Beyond Probability
quick_skim.abstract: This paper develops principled updating rules for possibilistic inference, where uncertainty about a fixed parameter is represented by a possibility function, the maxitive analogue of a probability distribution, and comparisons are made pointwise via a partial order. From two complementary foundations, an information-conservation viewpoint and an axiomatic viewpoint, we derive the same canonical update: the posterior is the prior-likelihood product followed by supremum normalisation. The two derivations agree for an arbitrary loss, differing only in where the learning-rate parameter enters. This parameter controls epistemic strength and is not identifiable from the normalising evidence alone, clarifying the role of analogous learning-rate parameters in generalised Bayesian updating.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: possibilistic inference rule akin to evidential reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可能性推理规则类似证据推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可能性推理规则类似证据推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可能性推理规则类似证据推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops principled updating rules for possibilistic inference from information-conservation and axiomatic foundations, providing a formal framework for rule-based inference under uncertainty.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文从信息守恒和公理化两个互补视角，为可能性推理推导出规范的更新规则：后验是先验与似然的乘积再经过上确界归一化。该规则与证据推理中的置信更新具有深层联系，为不确定性下的规则推理提供了形式化基础，可直接支持置信规则库的推理机制设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文从信息守恒和公理化两个互补视角，为可能性推理推导出规范的更新规则：后验是先验与似然的乘积再经过上确界归一化。该规则与证据推理中的置信更新具有深层联系，为不确定性下的规则推理提供了形式化基础，可直接支持置信规则库的推理机制设计。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: rule based inference methodology using evidential reasoning

quick_skim.title: From Neural Network Decisions to Training Cases: An Exact Account via Case-Based Decision Theory
quick_skim.abstract: Neural networks increasingly guide decisions in high-stakes domains such as medical diagnosis, credit approval, and energy bidding. Audit in these settings requires case-level evidence: which training cases support an action and what outcomes they carried. Case-based decision theory (CBDT) formalizes this reasoning by aggregating outcome support from remembered cases. We show that an OLS action readout fitted on a fixed neural representation admits an exact case-based decomposition. Each action score is a weighted sum of training-case returns, with coefficients determined by empirical Gram geometry. We identify a sufficient regime for CBDT similarity semantics; outside it, the coefficients should generally be treated as signed Gram-geometric influence. The decomposition yields audit signals that trace scores to training cases, measure action coherence, and identify weak support. Across synthetic CBDT, PJM, Adult Income, and Default Credit tasks, the method recovers case-level preference structure and achieves the highest mean Top-30 consistency among compared attribution baselines, while remaining competitive on support reconstruction. The audit requires only fitting an OLS top-layer probe, without retraining the representation or accessing the original optimization trajectory; probe fidelity is measured by score reconstruction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Case-based decision theory offers an alternative inference framework; relevant to rule-based inference methodologies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于案例的决策理论提供了一种替代推理框架；与基于规则的推理方法论相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于案例的决策理论提供了一种替代推理框架；与基于规则的推理方法论相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于案例的决策理论提供了一种替代推理框架；与基于规则的推理方法论相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that neural network decisions can be decomposed as a weighted sum of training case outcomes via case-based decision theory, providing an exact interpretable framework akin to rule-based inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文证明，对于固定神经网络表示上的线性决策层，其输出可以精确地分解为训练案例结果的加权和，从而建立了与基于案例的决策理论（CBDT）的等价关系。这一分解为神经网络决策提供了案例级别的可解释性，类似于基于规则的推理系统中每条规则的贡献度。虽然CBDT与置信规则库在形式上不同，但都为不确定性决策提供了透明、可解释的机制，对理解和改进BRB的推理过程具有启发意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文证明，对于固定神经网络表示上的线性决策层，其输出可以精确地分解为训练案例结果的加权和，从而建立了与基于案例的决策理论（CBDT）的等价关系。这一分解为神经网络决策提供了案例级别的可解释性，类似于基于规则的推理系统中每条规则的贡献度。虽然CBDT与置信规则库在形式上不同，但都为不确定性决策提供了透明、可解释的机制，对理解和改进BRB的推理过程具有启发意义。

quick_skim.title: Decision Making Needs Uncertainty Quantification [Lecture Notes]
quick_skim.abstract: Many signal processing systems ultimately exist to {act}. Whenever the state variable that determines the action to be taken by a decision maker, or agent, is uncertain, the way that uncertainty is represented decides how well the agent performs and how much its performance can be trusted. This lecture note develops, from first principles and within a single decision-theoretic setting, the link between the {objective} and the knowledge of an agent and the form of uncertainty representation that is sufficient to act optimally. To start, assuming a known environment distribution, we show that a risk-neutral agent needs the posterior distribution over the state, whereas a risk-averse agent can rely without loss of optimality on a {prediction set} and a worst-case decision rule. We then turn to the case in which the environment is unknown, and identify three complementary approaches to address the resulting epistemic uncertainty: calibration of a fixed predictor, credal (ambiguity) sets with distributionally robust optimization, and Bayesian inference over model parameters. The common thread is that reliable decisions require an uncertainty representation matched to the decision objective and to the knowledge profile of the agent, together with a guarantee that certifies the utility the agent will actually obtain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 决策中的不确定性量化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 决策中的不确定性量化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 决策中的不确定性量化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This lecture note links uncertainty representation to decision-making optimality for risk-neutral and risk-averse agents, offering foundational theory transferable to belief rule base uncertainty modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该讲义从第一性原理出发，在单一决策理论框架内建立了不确定性表示与决策最优性之间的联系。针对风险中性和风险厌恶的决策者，分别证明后验分布和预测集足以实现最优决策。为置信规则库中的不确定性建模提供了理论基础，有助于改进BRB在不确定环境下的推理性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该讲义从第一性原理出发，在单一决策理论框架内建立了不确定性表示与决策最优性之间的联系。针对风险中性和风险厌恶的决策者，分别证明后验分布和预测集足以实现最优决策。为置信规则库中的不确定性建模提供了理论基础，有助于改进BRB在不确定环境下的推理性能。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: uncertainty modeling in belief rule base
